name: nymphaea lotus
mechanism: Likely mediated in part by flavonoid/polyphenol antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated. May cause drowsiness at high doses. Avoid use with CNS depressants; Avoid with sedatives; Avoid with and alcohol; Avoid in pregnancy; Avoid combining with sedatives, generally well tolerated; Avoid use with CNS depressants or drowsiness at high doses; Avoid use during pregnancy unless advised by a clinician, alcohol or or other CNS depressants; Avoid combining with sedatives.

description: Nymphaea lotus is a medicinal herb traditionally used for relaxation and mild euphoria.
